reasoning: Without access to search tools or the specific Polymarket market URL, I am relying on base rate forecasting for FOMC meetings. The resolution date is far in the future (October 2025), so predicting the precise monetary policy action requires a structural estimate. Historically, at any given future meeting over a year out, the Fed has about a 50% chance of an interest rate pause (maintaining the rate), a 35% chance of a cut, and a 15% chance of a hike. When the Fed does cut rates, it typically does so in 25 basis point increments. Assuming a 35% chance of a rate cut and a 70% chance that any such cut is exactly 25 bps (as opposed to 50 bps or larger), the unconditional probability of a 25 bps cut is roughly 24-25%. Given the general expectation of a gradual easing cycle ending around late 2024 to early 2025, late 2025 might see fewer frequent cuts and more stable rates. I estimate the probability of exactly a 25 bps cut at the October 2025 meeting to be around 24%.
